intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present after-hours clinician who palpated a tender hepatomegaly and splenomegaly and checked for asterixis to exclude fulminant hepatic failure. The AI Doctor CANNOT palpate the abdomen, reliably assess asterixis, or draw bloods; it is scored on recognising the acute-hepatitis pattern plus the jaundice alarm, arranging an URGENT in-person assessment and urgent bloods (LFTs, coagulation/INR, viral hepatitis serology), and safety-netting hard for encephalopathy/coagulopathy — not on confirming the diagnosis remotely.

The source prints the diagnosis (Acute Hepatitis B), the clinician's 'serum sickness-like prodrome' interpretation, the ruled-out differentials, and a 'positive HBsAg is presumed pending serological confirmation' line UP FRONT. That diagnosis label, the interpretive prodrome phrase, the differential screen, and the anticipated confirmatory serology are the sealed answer key and are kept out of nodes 01/02.

T4/T5 ceiling discipline: normal vitals, no asterixis/confusion, no coagulopathy, source Risk Level Low (medical moderate) and disposition urgent bloods + GP review 24-48h. The uplift for an unexcludable fulminant-failure risk plus the palpation/bloods the AI cannot perform has a ceiling of T3 (urgent same-day in-person assessment) — there is no patient-reportable time-critical emergency feature at presentation (patient explicitly denies haematemesis/melaena; no confusion or bleeding). The escalation edges carry the 000/ED uplift for encephalopathy or coagulopathy if they appear.

No-dose discipline: the AI must not mint any drug dose; the clinical pharmacology answer is what to AVOID (paracetamol, NSAIDs, alcohol) in acute hepatocellular injury and NOT reflexively starting antivirals in uncomplicated acute HBV. No fabricated LFT/INR/serology numbers on the AI-readable side.

Premature-closure / red-herring probe: marked patient anxiety about the diagnosis is a psychosocial distractor; closing on 'just a viral illness / anxiety' without arranging the workup is the prime error of omission.
Source case: GIH-033 (Gastroenterology & Hepatology), primarily hepatobiliary — specialty HEPAT.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
